The Euphorbiaceae family is a diverse group of plants that includes over 2,800 species, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. intro: Zosterophyllanthos recurvatus, also known as the Australian finger lily, is a small succulent plant native to Australia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Zosterophyllanthos recurvatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
